name: Sarah DeValkenaere
subject: In prison for saving a life
body:
My husband is an honest man of integrity who followed in his father’s law enforcement footsteps. Eric himself is a decorated, 21-year veteran of the Kansas City Police Department. Dear Friend,

I wish I could sit down with you face to face instead of writing this e-mail.
[](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=f4ee2cf0b80b4b9db05d582af045686a&_e=2QE8CheyCAz6mIaD6uJxzkzu9oKPUbJfzPjjjOVaK83_lOXHlkjnAwml8rglN4XCICDNSTXZgSXlP2HZGWTaqK6f3FJRlj08xor9UPGc9QDYaBOtUCsjijWkv7p2P4i9Yd_V63OIwlpa42U8TAgQtWsQYRGN0ywJ1tYlVNWOSmUcTAf4uCyjMv31o9qBzibP1tko5Pj0amJWFz2jEcnmU3lrCcK9e13HNhabEJF9_lDquVnwigidnmdpknfoA9N3s66piiZDt7Mp0aQ4SiI-WaB3GX2WDpygllvXHXtbkkEKsZfhrj927CPsV_DK6PLwf-IhfOA16pkSHZu1dA3Uj9WEkLsr3hojdGgwiyhEu4l_9Hv5OSJuDZP05PURRU8gvFTq221ZeAyKN5Rfh_F4EA%3D%3D)
That's a picture of me and my husband, Eric, so you at least know what we look like.

Eric is a Detective with the Kansas City Police Department.

I wanted to send a picture of us with our three beautiful children - but in this current political climate, I don't feel safe sharing photos of our kids on the Internet.

When I tell you what happened, I think you'll understand. **[And I pray that you'll find it in your heart to help me save my husband.](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=f4ee2cf0b80b4b9db05d582af045686a&_e=2QE8CheyCAz6mIaD6uJxzkzu9oKPUbJfzPjjjOVaK83_lOXHlkjnAwml8rglN4XCICDNSTXZgSXlP2HZGWTaqK6f3FJRlj08xor9UPGc9QDYaBOtUCsjijWkv7p2P4i9Yd_V63OIwlpa42U8TAgQtWsQYRGN0ywJ1tYlVNWOSmUcTAf4uCyjMv31o9qBzibP1tko5Pj0amJWFz2jEcnmU3lrCcK9e13HNhabEJF9_lDquVnwigidnmdpknfoA9N3YVv3-2KhU8yqDYGfOAkLMVKMFmOcT2jKZQ3cwOH3mb31mA92n6QrxsjFgQwhwPy2LT0lpZc4nspSfhF99CJBKohs_lZBUlXpKF6ex286Klk_lQIidDDRUO3sx2qIbcC4DQHnn8hcMJ9nJM46rRG_3A%3D%3D)**

You see, it all started in early December - a few weeks before Christmas.

One of Eric's fellow detectives witnessed two vehicles speeding through the city streets - one was clearly chasing the other - and the police helicopter quickly got involved. A spotter in the helicopter was able to locate the suspect in a red pickup truck... still speeding down city streets.

The driver, 26-year-old Cameron Lamb, was chasing his ex-girlfriend in a stolen truck - weaving across lanes and running through stoplights.

My husband and his partner responded to the call and caught up with the suspect as he attempted to back the stolen truck into a garage.

Both detectives got out of their police vehicles and approached the stolen truck while repeatedly ordering the suspect to STOP and SHOW HIS HANDS.

That's when it happened.

**As Eric 's partner approached the driver's side, the suspect reached into his waistband and pulled out a handgun - pointing it directly at the detective.**

**My husband 's police training kicked in.**

He fired four rounds in the direction of the suspect through the truck's front windshield. The truck continued traveling into the garage where it finally came to a stop.

When officers entered, they found the suspect deceased - his arm hanging out the open window with a .45 semi-automatic pistol on the ground below.

That night, when Eric returned home, I hugged him and burst into tears. I was so thankful he was safe - and hopeful we could move forward and put this episode behind us.

But it didn't end there.

Several months later, the local prosecutor _charged Eric with MANSLAUGHTER and Armed Criminal Action._

I was in shock.

Then late last year, my husband finally went to trial. The judge ruled against him - and Eric was sentenced to 6 years in prison.

Now you understand why I'm asking for your help.

My husband is an honest man of integrity who followed in his father's law enforcement footsteps. Eric himself is a decorated, 21-year veteran of the Kansas City Police Department.

He's a role model in our community - a Little League baseball coach, a tireless volunteer, and a loving husband and father.

My husband is a hero.

But it didn't matter to the prosecutor that the suspect recklessly swerved a stolen truck through city streets... that he was violent toward his ex-girlfriend and endangered the lives of her two children... that he pointed a stolen gun directly at a police officer.

For serving our community for over 20 years - and risking his life - my has been shipped to an out-of-state prison.

And since the Missouri Supreme Court recently declined to take up Eric's case, that's where he'll stay for the next six years unless he receives a pardon from the Governor of Missouri. He'll miss our wedding anniversaries, our children's birthdays, their graduations(?), and our home will be left without the father and husband we love.

Even if Eric's good name is eventually cleared, just thinking about the cost of his defense and now our efforts to get him pardoned is overwhelming...

... but thank God we got a call from Jason Johnson, President of the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund (LELDF).

If you've ever heard of LELDF you know they're a national group fighting on behalf of wrongly accused police officers like Eric.

LELDF has helped dozens of innocent cops - and their families - at their lowest moments.

And with the thought of trying to suddenly raise our three children alone - I was certainly at one of the lowest moments of my life.

Not only did the LELDF board decide to help provide Eric with the best possible legal defense, but now they're helping us launch a nationwide petition campaign urging Missouri Governor Mike Parson to pardon Eric.

But as a non-profit organization, they depend solely on generous gifts from folks like you to defend officers like Eric.

I truly believe God is using Jason and the LELDF to answer our prayers.

That's because my husband and I are not wealthy people. I'm a kindergarten teacher and Eric is a career cop.

So I must ask you one of the toughest questions I've ever asked anyone.

**Will you donate[$35 or any amount to LELDF today to help save my husband?](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=f4ee2cf0b80b4b9db05d582af045686a&_e=2QE8CheyCAz6mIaD6uJxzkzu9oKPUbJfzPjjjOVaK83_lOXHlkjnAwml8rglN4XCICDNSTXZgSXlP2HZGWTaqK6f3FJRlj08xor9UPGc9QDYaBOtUCsjijWkv7p2P4i9Yd_V63OIwlpa42U8TAgQtWsQYRGN0ywJ1tYlVNWOSmUcTAf4uCyjMv31o9qBzibP1tko5Pj0amJWFz2jEcnmU3lrCcK9e13HNhabEJF9_lDquVnwigidnmdpknfoA9N32wmeHaXUx-4K2WQw4GtXsJ3yfX5WM7wVM4u9B74FPxObtKXyRWVR1dvT7-8bEATk_MGPrmSaVDLPp4V-7CElKp9Vv8pba3_BH_oc4ykZgLPiY3PoiFOtX5_HHo8W18lEHrP-k9da36veSGtM0QQeHw%3D%3D)**

If I had anywhere else to turn, I wouldn't be so bold as to ask for your help.

But I am so scared for Eric and I will do whatever I can for him - and for our children.

**[So I pray you'll find it in your heart today to make a tax-deductible gift of $35, $50, $100 or more to the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund.](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=f4ee2cf0b80b4b9db05d582af045686a&_e=2QE8CheyCAz6mIaD6uJxzkzu9oKPUbJfzPjjjOVaK83_lOXHlkjnAwml8rglN4XCICDNSTXZgSXlP2HZGWTaqK6f3FJRlj08xor9UPGc9QDYaBOtUCsjijWkv7p2P4i9Yd_V63OIwlpa42U8TAgQtWsQYRGN0ywJ1tYlVNWOSmUcTAf4uCyjMv31o9qBzibP1tko5Pj0amJWFz2jEcnmU3lrCcK9e13HNhabEJF9_lDquVnwigidnmdpknfoA9N3z3ucEmMOM-6sLnjMnYRjXY8t4kslEQEO18msiGtaa8Mf1d4HnNF3-gRIJ1qq7TjWAH9GJWDEg0iH38yYpXaVR0LgMc8n7A2UnJg1D8srwTVOyeux165k5psM7SLsh-cLLlMN8Bh9OYSto7XhKBwZTA%3D%3D)**

Eric and I - and our three kids - are counting on the good nature and generosity of kind neighbors like you to help bring Eric home where he belongs.

No matter what you're able to give today, please pray for us. Thank you in advance for your support.

Sincerely,

Sarah DeValkenaere

P.S. The Missouri Supreme Court has declined to take Eric's case. Now a pardon is one of the last remaining options to bring Eric home. We're feverishly trying to raise the money to pay for a massive petition drive urging the Missouri Governor Mike Parson to pardon Eric. I know my husband is innocent. But it's so hard to be a cop these days. The 'defund the police' movement has wiped out the support officers like Eric need to do their jobs.

And dozens of prosecutors across the country have made it a priority to try and lock up as many cops as they can. Even if they followed their training or defended themselves in the line of duty. The brave men and women in blue need to know that someone... anyone still has their back.

**[That's why I sincerely hope you'll help me get justice for Eric - and our entire family - by making a tax-deductible gift of any amount to the LELDF today.](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=f4ee2cf0b80b4b9db05d582af045686a&_e=2QE8CheyCAz6mIaD6uJxzkzu9oKPUbJfzPjjjOVaK83_lOXHlkjnAwml8rglN4XCICDNSTXZgSXlP2HZGWTaqK6f3FJRlj08xor9UPGc9QDYaBOtUCsjijWkv7p2P4i9Yd_V63OIwlpa42U8TAgQtWsQYRGN0ywJ1tYlVNWOSmUcTAf4uCyjMv31o9qBzibP1tko5Pj0amJWFz2jEcnmU3lrCcK9e13HNhabEJF9_lDquVnwigidnmdpknfoA9N3zOipnValrBi2M4dTgigLwZ9_BmyCU1bHzWdRwS5xVyrid2ovBYfwKS-QLa7a9cUrmjKInGkRNCo45uVJ1ALfCgfpDa4sBwEXoaZmAvIn2u5v2TMfd2_r5nYr_NEOlIgn4nYiDYJcCUQi9jqDFQ448Q%3D%3D)**

Thank you, and God bless!
--------

_More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we 're defending, please visit **www.PoliceDefense.org**. And remember -Blue Lives Matter!_

_(C) Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund | [www.PoliceDefense.org](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=f4ee2cf0b80b4b9db05d582af045686a&_e=2QE8CheyCAz6mIaD6uJxzkbmD7dB02UeWMXrYa94hHSgd82dNr3yB1ziiudDZKDFinJjZm3nswB6uLzzwAk0x96XIVb0P8ZsOxPHSnHCCovGMPnksoobO_BHzYX26lu1MHyxpZt-AmKoZ_zw7ZzMildTamk3GlLgHbDDG9nrTaXp6ifE5-EpSLYBFfN2sIuzwuCpI9LB1r_rfSPfpL21YQ%3D%3D)

clean_body: My husband is an honest man of integrity who followed in his father’s law enforcement footsteps. Eric himself is a decorated, 21-year veteran of the Kansas City Police Department. Dear Friend, I wish I could sit down with you face to face instead of writing this e-mail. That's a picture of me and my husband, Eric, so you at least know what we look like. Eric is a Detective with the Kansas City Police Department. I wanted to send a picture of us with our three beautiful children - but in this current political climate, I don't feel safe sharing photos of our kids on the Internet. When I tell you what happened, I think you'll understand. You see, it all started in early December - a few weeks before Christmas. One of Eric's fellow detectives witnessed two vehicles speeding through the city streets - one was clearly chasing the other - and the police helicopter quickly got involved. A spotter in the helicopter was able to locate the suspect in a red pickup truck... still speeding down city streets. The driver, 26-year-old Cameron Lamb, was chasing his ex-girlfriend in a stolen truck - weaving across lanes and running through stoplights. My husband and his partner responded to the call and caught up with the suspect as he attempted to back the stolen truck into a garage. Both detectives got out of their police vehicles and approached the stolen truck while repeatedly ordering the suspect to STOP and SHOW HIS HANDS. That's when it happened. As Eric 's partner approached the driver's side, the suspect reached into his waistband and pulled out a handgun - pointing it directly at the detective. My husband 's police training kicked in. He fired four rounds in the direction of the suspect through the truck's front windshield. The truck continued traveling into the garage where it finally came to a stop. When officers entered, they found the suspect deceased - his arm hanging out the open window with a .45 semi-automatic pistol on the ground below. That night, when Eric returned home, I hugged him and burst into tears. I was so thankful he was safe - and hopeful we could move forward and put this episode behind us. But it didn't end there. Several months later, the local prosecutor charged Eric with MANSLAUGHTER and Armed Criminal Action. I was in shock. Then late last year, my husband finally went to trial. The judge ruled against him - and Eric was sentenced to 6 years in prison. Now you understand why I'm asking for your help. My husband is an honest man of integrity who followed in his father's law enforcement footsteps. Eric himself is a decorated, 21-year veteran of the Kansas City Police Department. He's a role model in our community - a Little League baseball coach, a tireless volunteer, and a loving husband and father. My husband is a hero. But it didn't matter to the prosecutor that the suspect recklessly swerved a stolen truck through city streets... that he was violent toward his ex-girlfriend and endangered the lives of her two children... that he pointed a stolen gun directly at a police officer. For serving our community for over 20 years - and risking his life - my has been shipped to an out-of-state prison. And since the Missouri Supreme Court recently declined to take up Eric's case, that's where he'll stay for the next six years unless he receives a pardon from the Governor of Missouri. He'll miss our wedding anniversaries, our children's birthdays, their graduations(?), and our home will be left without the father and husband we love. Even if Eric's good name is eventually cleared, just thinking about the cost of his defense and now our efforts to get him pardoned is overwhelming... ... but thank God we got a call from Jason Johnson, President of the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund (LELDF). If you've ever heard of LELDF you know they're a national group fighting on behalf of wrongly accused police officers like Eric. LELDF has helped dozens of innocent cops - and their families - at their lowest moments. And with the thought of trying to suddenly raise our three children alone - I was certainly at one of the lowest moments of my life. Not only did the LELDF board decide to help provide Eric with the best possible legal defense, but now they're helping us launch a nationwide petition campaign urging Missouri Governor Mike Parson to pardon Eric. But as a non-profit organization, they depend solely on generous gifts from folks like you to defend officers like Eric. I truly believe God is using Jason and the LELDF to answer our prayers. That's because my husband and I are not wealthy people. I'm a kindergarten teacher and Eric is a career cop. So I must ask you one of the toughest questions I've ever asked anyone. Will you donate If I had anywhere else to turn, I wouldn't be so bold as to ask for your help. But I am so scared for Eric and I will do whatever I can for him - and for our children. Eric and I - and our three kids - are counting on the good nature and generosity of kind neighbors like you to help bring Eric home where he belongs. No matter what you're able to give today, please pray for us. Thank you in advance for your support. Sincerely, Sarah DeValkenaere P.S. The Missouri Supreme Court has declined to take Eric's case. Now a pardon is one of the last remaining options to bring Eric home. We're feverishly trying to raise the money to pay for a massive petition drive urging the Missouri Governor Mike Parson to pardon Eric. I know my husband is innocent. But it's so hard to be a cop these days. The 'defund the police' movement has wiped out the support officers like Eric need to do their jobs. And dozens of prosecutors across the country have made it a priority to try and lock up as many cops as they can. Even if they followed their training or defended themselves in the line of duty. The brave men and women in blue need to know that someone... anyone still has their back. Thank you, and God bless! More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we 're defending, please visit www.PoliceDefense.org. And remember -Blue Lives Matter! (C) Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund | 2560 Huntington Ave. Suite 203, Alexandria, VA, 22303

name: Byron Donalds
subject: Cool as a cucumber
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Byron Donalds for Congress
body:
| | | | [](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9xeW/?__vbtrk=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
---
[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9xeW/?__vbtrk=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
|
---
I’m as cool as a cucumber.
You have to be when the Fake News is calling our MAGA Movement “evil” on national television.
This is Black MAGA Patriot Byron Donalds.
I got real tired of seeing them lying about us with no repercussions whatsoever.
So I thought to myself, why not just go on their shows, dismantle their lies, and set the record straight _**one-on-one**_?
That’s exactly what I did. This whole election, I’ve been one of the few MAGA Congressman to destroy their narratives LIVE - all thanks to folks like yourself.
But I just went on MSNBC & what I heard made me realize we still have _**[so much work to do.](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9xeW/?__vbtrk=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**_
They interrogated me, demanding to know if I thought that President-Elect Trump was too dangerous to be in the White House, then even hinted that he wasn’t qualified to be President.
**I kept my cool and picked their phony narratives apart piece by piece like clockwork.**
Problem is, they’ll keep trying to push this nonsense every single day until he’s inaugurated, and if no one challenges them, their lies could seriously get out of hand.
I know the election is over, but we have to remain on the offensive all the way through the inauguration, and it’s your generous support that keeps me in the fight.
_**[So please, can you chip into Fake News Offensive Fund today?](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9xeW/?__vbtrk=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**_ _**[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9xeX/?__vbtrk=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**__**[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9xeX/?__vbtrk=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)**_

|
---
[CHIP IN HERE](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9xeW/?__vbtrk=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjYyNjY0OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

Byron Donalds

name: Voter Action Project
subject: The gift of voting rights
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project Logo5 [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=120124_3&amount=25]
Team,
Giving Tuesday is right around the corner, and we cannot think of a better gift for everyone than protecting our right to vote.
We're not going to lie -- we know things look bleak. Opponents of voting rights had a good Election Day. We're staring down the barrel of more suppressive policies. That's no reason to give up, however, and we can't afford to throw our hands up and obey in advance. Not with the court cases, state bills, new laws, special elections, and so much more on the horizon.
We've set our goal to raise $50,000 from grassroots donors to help mobilize and defend voting rights before state legislative sessions start next year.
Will you chip in $25 or more to help reach our must-hit $50,000 fundraising goal before December 31? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=120124_3&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=120124_3&amount=15]
GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=120124_3&amount=25]
GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=120124_3&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=120124_3&amount=100]
GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?express_lane=true&refcode=120124_3&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=120124_3&amount=35]
Voter Action Project was founded to expand mail-in ballot access during the pandemic.
When voting rights were threatened in the wake of the 2020 election, we mobilized to fight back against the wave of voter suppression that swept across the country with more than 400 bills in 49 of 50 states.
Since then we've been on the front lines of every battle for our right to vote, from Congressional legislation to court cases to voter registration to voter turnout in the last two elections. We've helped countless voters register, find their polling place, get informed about the issues, and make their ballot count.
Simply put: There's no way we're giving up now. Not for a second. Not ever.
Team -- to accomplish these goals we need everyone with us. Fuel our efforts for Giving Tuesday and more with $25 or whatever you can give today >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-democracy?refcode=120124_3&amount=25]
http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=120124_3&amount=25 [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=120124_3&amount=25]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://twitter.com/voteractionproj [https://twitter.com/voteractionproj] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=120124_3&amount=25]
Donate Now [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-weekly?refcode=120124_3&amount=25]

This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=aSjZ7YExI8%2FBp0rhN49oJVIA7Z%2FYljd7v2STml%2BYkt7VifFG%2FFSJ0cq%2FGjws83Pba1ddKHcbWA5Pe6kcMIN3rKnO97ee%2F%2FWtNTKqHtul9%2BqAXEYeunYBS2fb8Q3NrwPlL3be870YJXyiqqPUld%2FZs0qrgDbj4aU1Iza4drskvE3j8hl7PuqM8%2BzcZwSfCa1nyGOUHMcKCkeKexpx76zvdZSdbxkvi7aUAdAvaIE1TV%2FehVt26UGaPLuSV3MIBpUCVuWGe8STf7jBDmMH5l8OsXA%2FD1n9PCtjdQFAvwq5n6m4pG3ZqC0LFpRMQz9ClMsNRpjapM96Aqa8pXFicvrS991mzPAtapxlGM3t%2Bzbzy%2B4DCDI8LphFz9co41YiOlF6LOpJ01WQjg%2FS9d53nryopa7cWQXWaJZBqHvCPjgqa4g%3D .
clean_body: Voter Action Project Logo5 [] Team, Giving Tuesday is right around the corner, and we cannot think of a better gift for everyone than protecting our right to vote. We're not going to lie -- we know things look bleak. Opponents of voting rights had a good Election Day. We're staring down the barrel of more suppressive policies. That's no reason to give up, however, and we can't afford to throw our hands up and obey in advance. Not with the court cases, state bills, new laws, special elections, and so much more on the horizon. We've set our goal to raise $50,000 from grassroots donors to help mobilize and defend voting rights before state legislative sessions start next year. Will you chip in $25 or more to help reach our must-hit $50,000 fundraising goal before December 31? >> [] If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [] Other Amount [] Voter Action Project was founded to expand mail-in ballot access during the pandemic. When voting rights were threatened in the wake of the 2020 election, we mobilized to fight back against the wave of voter suppression that swept across the country with more than 400 bills in 49 of 50 states. Since then we've been on the front lines of every battle for our right to vote, from Congressional legislation to court cases to voter registration to voter turnout in the last two elections. We've helped countless voters register, find their polling place, get informed about the issues, and make their ballot count. Simply put: There's no way we're giving up now. Not for a second. Not ever. Team -- to accomplish these goals we need everyone with us. Fuel our efforts for Giving Tuesday and more with $25 or whatever you can give today >> [] [] [] [] [] [] Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [] Donate Now [] Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code. A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003. Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. Integrity First Voter Action Project P.O. Box 19294 Portland, OR 97280 United States

name: House GOP Alerts
subject: Will the liberal elites silence our voices and erase our achievements?
body:
| Liquid error: Internal error Liquid error: Internal error Liquid error: Internal error Liquid error: Internal error Fellow Conservative, As we celebrate our historic victory in the 2024 election, we cannot forget that our work is far from over. With our GOP Majority at risk in battleground races nationwide, every single grassroots Republican must stand up and be counted. We saw it on election night - the energy, the passion, and the determination of our supporters propelled us to victory. But now, we must channel that same energy into defending our Majority and ensuring that our conservative values continue to shape the future of America. The liberal elites and their allies in the media will stop at nothing to undermine our progress and discredit our leadership. We've seen it already - from **[biased reporting](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=57066749a81d428c9d052b872c19eedd&_e=EufYxOjmuyy4PRW_XV9-cSx4-BSZsxyEGIW4MDIfP6WF1qjleBaNxw5VQm__BDP7QbIWOwDYcnre5WKHDvEMCSMotSzD83R4luRns0VGPU0qV8ae4m4ArRza03SexRh5Z3muRQX5b5UtoOzIN7WCG3WuqGB-RnEr29BFx9ExTLk-VdoY0XJbtla_lQqX7aGc_0LIKimC3eLQzvq7nUgcv38dW_QUpf8ZLD5-Lms_xrbfM8-X9LiEpc8Npf5q1WDRXhr_6qaMm5RCH4O-g-CSpQ_7p76a48WNne5dj7krjmBI09NP3QoD-pDh8es9MWFGcKAqoKabzD4zDm1dNGDo_sLeHhVueyKdP8n5bIGJ3CODmnLN7_o_cfVUT9gicXvo4rvhtQdprn5OR-_E-B9EJs_ZpX4w_IMUO8vnp17BQwUBW8r07sxGOWXpFDCQ53Uqfjn_LXDxehDiMtbjSLZ2RF-S0eTy2drQvA8dcTuUPFwVYNBh78VVQRg7uolFx93_uz2O7w9OJOvb_6354C2pYl_AEqnEmPM8CNyYJ7YgNLDquT0P2YMAcxPCY_rtWAAOkm4l45Qfrd-UpsXyK3gxhEvnxJ_FTDzz0J98FGH36ZtYw2vmBXnEGJTn2J3rdobQkaMCQjTr4xnYLwvRKXE-DQ%3D%3D)** to [outright lies](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=57066749a81d428c9d052b872c19eedd&_e=EufYxOjmuyy4PRW_XV9-cSx4-BSZsxyEGIW4MDIfP6WF1qjleBaNxw5VQm__BDP7QbIWOwDYcnre5WKHDvEMCSMotSzD83R4luRns0VGPU0qV8ae4m4ArRza03SexRh5Z3muRQX5b5UtoOzIN7WCG3WuqGB-RnEr29BFx9ExTLk-VdoY0XJbtla_lQqX7aGc_0LIKimC3eLQzvq7nUgcv38dW_QUpf8ZLD5-Lms_xrbfM8-X9LiEpc8Npf5q1WDRXhr_6qaMm5RCH4O-g-CSpQ_7p76a48WNne5dj7krjmBI09NP3QoD-pDh8es9MWFGcKAqoKabzD4zDm1dNGDo_sLeHhVueyKdP8n5bIGJ3CODmnLN7_o_cfVUT9gicXvo4rvhtQdprn5OR-_E-B9EJs_ZpX4w_IMUO8vnp17BQwUBW8r07sxGOWXpFDCQ53Uqfjn_LXDxehDiMtbjSLZ2RF-S0eTy2drQvA8dcTuUPFylGOT5qunGyPYvY0U_ajaAFtGjuMIj7Y_WDSvg9amt-cG30-7QNHUfe5YTr4OXfvXZznHgCBqIU3Q8ocbMrSZ77yYAB8LoXmEsrag_WoSU0OiuWke7FqWaMvYeOGliBMeCVbAHBBZtT9eDZf-yfypOVJnunWtEAymkG8G06rqAMg%3D%3D), they'll do whatever it takes to erode our support and regain power. But we won't let them. We won't let them _[silence our voices](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=57066749a81d428c9d052b872c19eedd&_e=EufYxOjmuyy4PRW_XV9-cSx4-BSZsxyEGIW4MDIfP6WF1qjleBaNxw5VQm__BDP7QbIWOwDYcnre5WKHDvEMCSMotSzD83R4luRns0VGPU0qV8ae4m4ArRza03SexRh5Z3muRQX5b5UtoOzIN7WCG3WuqGB-RnEr29BFx9ExTLk-VdoY0XJbtla_lQqX7aGc_0LIKimC3eLQzvq7nUgcv38dW_QUpf8ZLD5-Lms_xrbfM8-X9LiEpc8Npf5q1WDRXhr_6qaMm5RCH4O-g-CSpQ_7p76a48WNne5dj7krjmBI09NP3QoD-pDh8es9MWFGcKAqoKabzD4zDm1dNGDo_sLeHhVueyKdP8n5bIGJ3CODmnLN7_o_cfVUT9gicXvo4rvhtQdprn5OR-_E-B9EJs_ZpX4w_IMUO8vnp17BQwUBW8r07sxGOWXpFDCQ53Uqfjn_LXDxehDiMtbjSLZ2RF-S0eTy2drQvA8dcTuUPFw-L-W6Ax7IC15O6jP2s1elar9fs0KrAmBuqCIEbRXiJz6IJKi4FsbvOuhHoIJhY4XxLIeE5_o9w_MTHeQId5YMEUvxoiwnUFFJyB2-I-1SftjFak42LZPy9NAh8jhIWmJ2z43lzLwzugZcxWvq9xkSwg_CrPG-RkVsGXhfddA6vA%3D%3D)_ , dismiss our values, or erase our achievements. We'll stand strong, united, and resolute in our commitment to America's greatness. That's why I'm asking you to **[contribute $15 or more immediately](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=57066749a81d428c9d052b872c19eedd&_e=EufYxOjmuyy4PRW_XV9-cSx4-BSZsxyEGIW4MDIfP6WF1qjleBaNxw5VQm__BDP7QbIWOwDYcnre5WKHDvEMCSMotSzD83R4luRns0VGPU0qV8ae4m4ArRza03SexRh5Z3muRQX5b5UtoOzIN7WCG3WuqGB-RnEr29BFx9ExTLk-VdoY0XJbtla_lQqX7aGc_0LIKimC3eLQzvq7nUgcv38dW_QUpf8ZLD5-Lms_xrbfM8-X9LiEpc8Npf5q1WDRXhr_6qaMm5RCH4O-g-CSpQ_7p76a48WNne5dj7krjmBI09NP3QoD-pDh8es9MWFGcKAqoKabzD4zDm1dNGDo_sLeHhVueyKdP8n5bIGJ3CODmnLN7_o_cfVUT9gicXvo4rvhtQdprn5OR-_E-B9EJs_ZpX4w_IMUO8vnp17BQwUBW8r07sxGOWXpFDCQ53Uqfjn_LXDxehDiMtbjSLZ2RF-S0eTy2drQvA8dcTuUPFwVYNBh78VVQRg7uolFx93_YZpBUiwUwIdC9VzioHF2qyLwTsH7P_MlGjpmJ0SHW5JEZL1Ji3T3kuWMxV1CZ1PkX8-KManA95CuEMkSgbYp6tD61YRkDg8Srnai6b38aqsBaYv2eZANbUhbEoYHky5LgQanecN3EdJ1HBZeCB4itQ%3D%3D)** to help us defend our Majority and uphold conservative leadership. Your support will fuel our efforts to mobilize voters, amplify our message, and hold liberal Democrats accountable for their failed policies. This is our moment, Fellow Conservative. This is our chance to make a difference, to shape the course of history, and to ensure that America remains a beacon of freedom and opportunity for generations to come. Stand with us today. **[Contribute $15 or more immediately](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=57066749a81d428c9d052b872c19eedd&_e=EufYxOjmuyy4PRW_XV9-cSx4-BSZsxyEGIW4MDIfP6WF1qjleBaNxw5VQm__BDP7QbIWOwDYcnre5WKHDvEMCSMotSzD83R4luRns0VGPU0qV8ae4m4ArRza03SexRh5Z3muRQX5b5UtoOzIN7WCG3WuqGB-RnEr29BFx9ExTLk-VdoY0XJbtla_lQqX7aGc_0LIKimC3eLQzvq7nUgcv38dW_QUpf8ZLD5-Lms_xrbfM8-X9LiEpc8Npf5q1WDRXhr_6qaMm5RCH4O-g-CSpQ_7p76a48WNne5dj7krjmBI09NP3QoD-pDh8es9MWFGcKAqoKabzD4zDm1dNGDo_sLeHhVueyKdP8n5bIGJ3CODmnLN7_o_cfVUT9gicXvo4rvhtQdprn5OR-_E-B9EJs_ZpX4w_IMUO8vnp17BQwUBW8r07sxGOWXpFDCQ53Uqfjn_LXDxehDiMtbjSLZ2RF-S0eTy2drQvA8dcTuUPFwVYNBh78VVQRg7uolFx93_bS4OeRFpfCHotGvLy5B6EyQv-9LMHRWXoCoE2q-BspXWfi5LTkJAqOzFIdhwykKR9K1CnDXmkmskM-8O8s6QdkWoSTaKjBZyXRLRL4TuEHrirbJgsGSBORkkwU_O-xU5psDUma9zBSvoPIAGetQxxA%3D%3D)**. |

body:
we’re counting on you to guide our agenda

Fellow Patriot, two things:

1.) Thank you for always being a steadfast conservative. Thanks to you, we kept Texas [RED](http://links.makingamericathegreatest.com/u/click?_t=79fa4a3daf024a0a886c5b498ff32e32&_m=9f81178db91d43cdad5dda683af8aec9&_e=fcIXRgwiVnU2r2egBRPgsX94ZXMDN8qoiKsfwcbgamZI07ln8M2YtZ17jPWRh4v97_CCPfH-ArYWRbu68E3UKc28Wbs12ZwOu3EkOZI03_twV4u8cShTLIAA5veoNoXjGvxLf4UyO5lDVv0qfek5t575Kw7I4MsucjgSMH1bzB_5nUTwmkiF4h1E80xgIHhY6o--b18TLAj8BRJlpJ7zEEadjPVzBiWVVOiTcAcqmXTVKJ6EQ0pxleo5DblJ4pO4RBYJxhJwhAVVF-iWOgLqpd8AzRSvuqSgLFhgQYWiOUv9rXIF4EWEezU6sTVRYvbwWls7Al8NRIvSjsBkh1Py9zxQE_EKiCyxh11AgXRW2OxdCiey4rOoK3wKc-RxIp-jKVYpjFCNQHyBLhyXSMDA60A2PVXx36fg7aK1W0d-5T_6_M-tV9BPK0E57tU4ejzTINTJ_mOB2yjozYgwOhuV_pz3EBs15OXnRDYSB3gvKfU%3D) and won back the Senate Majority.

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: Sunday News Digest: 12/1/2024
body:
Kamala’s campaign debt & Denver’s $356M immigration tab
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**December 1, 2024** **SUNDAY NEWS DIGEST** **By Heidi Ganahl**
| | Dear Readers,
This week, the spotlight is on accountability—or the lack of it—in leadership, with Vice President Kamala Harris facing mounting criticism from within her own party. As calls grow for an **audit of her campaign's $20 million debt** , Democrats are openly questioning her financial management and leadership in the aftermath of her electoral defeat. Instead of introspection, Harris has issued what some have called a "self-congratulatory review," fueling frustration among her allies and leaving a fractured party searching for answers.
This lack of awareness is emblematic of a broader issue in leadership. When leaders fail to confront hard truths, gaps in priorities and execution become glaring.
Denver’s fiscal woes present a parallel narrative. **Sanctuary city policies** have cost the city a staggering [$356 million over the past two years](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) to accommodate an influx of nearly 43,000 migrants. The strain on resources—from education to healthcare to housing—is a stark example of how unchecked spending without clear accountability can destabilize local governments. Both stories serve as a cautionary tale about what happens when priorities are misaligned and leadership fails to address root causes.
Let’s continue to hold our leaders accountable and strive for solutions that align with the values of transparency and responsibility.

In liberty and truth,
Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
| | **TOP STORIES OF THE WEEK**
---
**[The irony of Colorado obtaining gray wolves from British Columbia](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) While Colorado wildlife managers have been trying to figure out where to get the next crop of 10 to 15 wolves to import into Colorado, British Columbia, Canada, has been trying to cull their population of wolves because of the predation of caribou that led to the endangerment of the herds.

**[Schumann: Colorado Springs has ‘a hometown Jussie Smollett situation’. A look at a mayor’s alleged deception](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Colorado Springs is dealing with “a hometown Jussie Smollett situation.” That’s how Todd Watkins, vice chair of the El Paso County Republican Party, described the hate crime hoax involving Mayor Yemi Mobolade.

**[Kalam: Denver Mayor Mike Johnston’s insurrection rhetoric](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) In the wake of the recent electoral landslide — a decisive repudiation of unchecked illegal immigration and the catastrophic security failures at America’s borders — one might have expected local leaders to reconsider their stances on sanctuary city policies.

|
---
**STATE**
**[Recount ordered in House District 19; Results of HD16 and HD19 recounts due Dec. 6](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) The second of two recounts which will decide whether Republicans have erased the Democratic supermajority in the Colorado House has been ordered by Secretary of State Jena Griswold’s office.

**[Why are Colorado rest stops closed? CDOT blames a lack of funds to reopen](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Cass Brock of Pueblo is disappointed, to say the least, concerning access to the multiple rest stops along the Interstate 25 corridor from Denver to the southern part of the state.

**[Food Bank of the Rockies facing challenge of meeting highest food insecurity level in a decade](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) “On the Western Slope, one in eight people — our neighbors — are food insecure. For children, it’s even worse: one in seven kids is unsure where their next meal will come from,” shares Sue Ellen Rodwick, director of the Western Slope Food Bank of the Rockies. “Statewide, the numbers are slightly better, but still troubling. One in nine Coloradans face food insecurity.”
**[Proposed state budget could cut $110 million from already pothole-laden highways](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [THE SUM & SUBSTANCE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Colorado’s ability to build and fix the highways that are key to transporting goods and people could take a $110 million hit in the next fiscal year under budget-balancing proposals being offered by Gov. Jared Polis.
_**[READ MORE STATE NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**NATIONAL**
**[Canada, Mexico to face 25% tariffs, China 10% until borders are secure, drug importation stops, Trump says](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) President-elect Donald Trump has put Canada and Mexico on notice — close their borders with the United States or face the penalty of tariffs on all imports into the United States.

**[Tom Homan responds to Denver mayor: ‘He’s willing to go to jail, I’m willing to put him in jail’](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[FOX NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Democratic Denver Mayor Mike Johnston recently said he was prepared to go to jail over his opposition to the Trump administration’s border policies. The president-elect’s pick to be the next border czar responded that he’s willing to put him there.

**[Elon threatens to buy MSNBC and the internet cheers](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[MIG REPORTS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Recent reports suggest Comcast is preparing to sell MSNBC after increasingly dramatic ratings casualties post-election. Elon Musk, who has become infamous for purchasing Twitter in 2022, is making hay of the situation by joking about buying MSNBC.
**[Special counsel Jack Smith seeks to dismiss Trump’s January 6 case](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[NATIONAL REVIEW](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Biden-Harris Justice Department special counsel Jack Smith filed a motion Monday to dismiss all felony charges against President-elect Donald Trump in relation to the January 6 election-interference case.

**[READ MORE NATIONAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**LOCAL**
**[Mesa County makes progress in eradicating Japanese beetle from Western Slope](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Mesa County commissioners are breathing just a little bit easier after discovering that their efforts, combined with resources from the state and the community, are working to get rid of the Japanese beetle infestation on the Western Slope.

**[Illegal immigrant surges in Denver have cost $356 million, study finds](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [DENVER GAZETTE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) A new study estimated that the nearly 43,000 immigrants who have come to Denver over the past two years have cost $356 million. Conducted by the Common Sense Institute (CSI), the estimate examined the outlays by the city of Denver, as well as associated health care and education costs across the region.
**[Casa Bonita’s employees are unionizing. What are they hoping to gain?](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[FOX 31 NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) Onstage and backstage workers of the oft-noted Denver restaurant Casa Bonita have voted unanimously to unionize with two unions: The Actors’ Equity Association and IATSE Local 7.

**[Cañon City voted to build a new swimming pool, but not to fund it](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[THE COLORADO SUN](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) When voters delivered a mixed decision on building a new pool in town, they created a dilemma: build it without the assurance of having the money to operate it or walk away from the project despite voters’ approval of the sales tax and debt to build it.

**_[READ MORE LOCAL NEWS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)_**
|
---
**COMMENTARY**
**[Daniel: Let local communities lead on public lands](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**[ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _Public lands are woven into the fabric of Colorado’s identity, serving as living symbols of our shared heritage, natural beauty and deep connection to the land._ __
**[Boddie: Independent expenditure committees effect election outcomes](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _One of the reasons candidates lost in the 2024 election may be that independent expenditure committees (IECs), with millions to spend, negatively affected the races._
**[Gaines: Yes, oppose anti-2A legislation, but don’t forget to support state and local pro-gun orgs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _The recent election results for Proposition KK (the state excise tax on guns and ammunition) were disappointing, but not altogether a surprise._
**[Rahn: The value of AP African-American studies is in the truth, not CRT](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) _What is education for? Education is about searching for the truth, analyzing and differentiating good ideas from bad. Imagine a classroom where students are inspired to be creative, independent, taught self-determination and invested in the power of capitalism._
_**[READ MORE COMMENTARY](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**_
|
---
**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming a RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [brian@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:brian@rockymountainvoice.com)) or send us a message on social media to chat!
|
---
**🤔 DID YOU KNOW?** George Washington, the first U.S. president and Revolutionary War hero, was an **enthusiastic dog breeder** , particularly of hunting hounds, to which he gave names like “Sweet Lips” and “Drunkard.”
|

The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** “Gratitude is riches. Complaint is poverty.” — Doris Day
|

President Joe Biden talks with his son Hunter Biden upon arrival at Delaware Air National Guard Base in New Castle, Del., on June 11, 2024, as he travels to Wilmington, Del. (Andrew Caballero-Reynolds/AFP via Getty Images)
By Illinois Review

On Sunday, President Joe Biden issued a full pardon to his son Hunter – less than two months before he is set to leave office – becoming the first White House occupant to grant executive clemency to a child despite earlier promises to not intervene.

At a June press conference at the G7 summit in Italy, Biden said he would not exercise his presidential powers to pardon his son Hunter – stating that he will “abide by the jury decision” and that “I will not pardon him.”

But on Sunday, The White House issued a statement from the president stating his son was “selectively, and unfairly prosecuted” and that he “signed a pardon for my son Hunter.”

name: Big Trump Knife
subject: Introducing The 10" BIG Trump Knife 2024
body:
You need this massive statement-making fixed blade knife...
| | | [](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/19260/6374453/9d29e5e9c13657a3a289f48f7886b7d5eddaafd4/53658b857275dbeadad8374e69955c53a1b49287)
---
| | |
---
_December 2nd, 2024_
---
| | |
---
---
If you believe in **“Taking America Back”** with President Donald Trump in 2024, then you need this massive statement-making fixed blade knife.

The BIG Trump Knife is [laser-etched on a stainless steel blade with a waving American Flag and a stainless steel half-guard keeps your fingers from slipping.](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/19260/6374453/9d29e5e9c13657a3a289f48f7886b7d5eddaafd4/53658b857275dbeadad8374e69955c53a1b49287)
---
[](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/19260/6374453/9d29e5e9c13657a3a289f48f7886b7d5eddaafd4/53658b857275dbeadad8374e69955c53a1b49287)
---
[](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/19260/6374453/9d29e5e9c13657a3a289f48f7886b7d5eddaafd4/53658b857275dbeadad8374e69955c53a1b49287)
---
The patriotic handle scales are crafted of a **[blue pakkawood panel that has“Take America Back” in white, surrounded by white stars, and a red andwhite-striped acrylic panel.](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/19260/6374453/9d29e5e9c13657a3a289f48f7886b7d5eddaafd4/53658b857275dbeadad8374e69955c53a1b49287)**

The fixed blade knife comes with a belt sheath for ease of carry.

**Get Your Trump Knife Today &
Let Folks Know Where You Stand**
The Limited Edition Trump Knife is not just a rare collectible, but a symbol of your support for President Trump, who always stood for "True American" freedom.
---
[](https://links.gunowner-news.com/a/1537/click/19260/6374453/9d29e5e9c13657a3a289f48f7886b7d5eddaafd4/53658b857275dbeadad8374e69955c53a1b49287)

name: Beth Poole, Evergreen
subject: BIG climate news: A chance to Trump-proof our wins
disclaimer_text: paid for by Evergreen Action
body:
We can TRIPLE the funds for our all-out sprint to protect climate progress.
| | | | [](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3086966/87SdQ4wZ) |
---|---|---
---
| Friend,

As you may know, right now Evergreen Action is leading an **all-out sprint to Trump-proof climate wins** we've worked so hard to achieve over the last four years.

We have to work fast and make the most of every dollar and hour. But I'm thrilled to share that, **in order to help us put everything we've got into the last 50 days of this sprint,**[**a generous donor is now offering to triple all donations to Evergreen Action from now through #GivingTuesday.**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3086967/87SdQ4wZ)

What can we possibly do to stop Trump now, after such a bruising election? It might surprise you, but **there is actually a LOT we can save. Yes, even and _especially_ now.** From getting President Biden to deny fossil fuel project permits to quickly finalizing pending state climate regulations, we have drawn up a blueprint of bold, achievable actions that will, by design, be incredibly hard for Trump and his clown-car of climate change deniers to undo.

The truth is, we'll never have the unlimited budgets of the fossil-fuel giants salivating over Trump's win. But here at Evergreen Action, we don't need them to make a huge difference. Our team of policy experts and seasoned campaigners can make every cent of our budget count in a big way for the climate through strategic policy victories. [**So if you want to make it as hard as possible for Trump to tank our climate wins in 2025 and beyond, this is the time. Please have your gift tripled right now.**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3086968/87SdQ4wZ) |
---
[GIVE TODAY](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3086969/87SdQ4wZ)
---
---
| [**Your tripled gift**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3086970/87SdQ4wZ) will help us enact our three-pronged strategy to Trump-proof the wins we urgently need to preserve to head off the most damaging of climate scenarios. Here's our plan:

1. **Step 1: Halt pending fossil fuel projects before inauguration day.** Our first target is six super-polluting liquefied natural gas projects that Biden has the power to stop right now.
2. **Step 2: Speed through approvals for groundbreaking state climate project** s and make sure that governors, communities, and allies are prepared for states to lead the charge on climate in the coming presidency.
3. **Step 3: Build cross-movement power and solidarity.** Whether it is supporting litigation in state and federal court, holding corporations accountable, or working with champions in Congress to defend the Inflation Reduction Act, we are working to prepare our allies with smart strategy and strong coalitions now.

|
---
| Here at Evergreen, we know smart climate policy is policy that _sticks._ The blueprint is there. Now we need to get loud to make it happen while we have the chance.

Let's get it done together. [**Have your gift to protect the climate tripled today.**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/3086971/87SdQ4wZ)

In solidarity, |
---
| | |
---
### Beth Poole
Senior Fundraising Director
Evergreen Action
---
---
---
---
| | [ ](https://evergreenaction.com/)
---

name: Kirk Cameron
subject: Stop the indoctrination of our children
body:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/vsHBcr_WNp
As someone who's lived and worked in Hollywood my whole life, I
want to say:
http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/p/osHBcr_WNp

You are
absolutely right if you think Hollywood is reaching right into
your family living room with "entertainment" that grooms
children.

Fellow
Conservative,
As
someone who's lived and worked in Hollywood my whole life, I want
to say: You are absolutely right if you think Hollywood is
reaching right into your family living room with "entertainment"
that grooms children.
Today
when children turn on the TV, they see a Drag Queen leading the
Pride Parade on Blue's Clues...

And the
result of this constant propaganda? An upside-down world where
children lecture you on what you can and cannot say…
...and
they try to "educate" YOU about your "old-fashioned" and "mean"
ideas.
But you
and I know your ideas aren't old-fashioned or mean at all. They
are the same biblical values that made America the greatest
country on earth.
That's
why I'm reaching out to you. Today you have a very special chance
to stop the Hollywood groomers who are turning our children and
grandchildren against us and the Godly values we love.
Today,
I'm inviting you to be a Founding Supporter of the very first
children's television show that actively fights back against the
groomers in Hollywood AND reinforces the traditional biblical
values that made America great.
And it's
only possible with the help of motivated Christians who want to
see a Christian resurgence in America like you.

Introducing: Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk.
This
ground-breaking live-action television show captivates
children...
...but
more importantly, it builds their character at the same time.
Imagine a
children's show that leaves your child or grandchild with a
greater sense of right and wrong and an understanding of truth.
Courage,
honesty, discernment, forgiveness, empathy, all taught through a
biblical and traditional lens.
Imagine a
children's show that brings families together instead of driving
them apart.
Now
imagine YOU are the one who makes that show possible.
Because I
can't beat the Hollywood groomers without your help as a Founding
Supporter.
Season
One of Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk has ten lesson-filled
episodes planned.
And each
episode costs around $70,000 to produce.
I know
that's a lot of money but it's actually a fraction of what it
would cost if we made our show with a major Hollywood studio.
And
obviously we won't work through Hollywood.
Hollywood
has done quite enough already to destroy American culture and
hijack our children.
We
parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens must take over to
rebuild a thriving, healthy, GOOD and GODLY culture.
That's
why I'm asking you to contribute $35, $50, $250, $500, or more to
help us reach our fundraising goal of $70,000 per episode.
Whether
you can send $35, $50,000, or anything in between, or anything
higher or lower -- please know that gifts of EVERY size are
needed and greatly appreciated.
As your
special "Thank You" for joining me as a Founding Supporter at the
$35 and above level, we'll send you all ten episodes of
Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk for your family to enjoy FREE
on DVD.
Hollywood
groomers have destroyed America's Godly culture. But today we
rebuild from the ashes.
And it
starts by ensuring the most innocent among us are filled with
faith and family -- not filth.
Thank you
for joining us as a Founding Supporter.

Kirk
Cameron
Actor,
Author, and Father

Kirk
Cameron
Actor,
Author, and Father

DONATE

Brave
Books
13618
Poplar Cir, Suite 302
Conroe,
TX 77304

Courage, honesty, discernment, forgiveness, empathy, all taught through a biblical and traditional lens. Imagine a children's show that brings families together instead of driving them apart. Now imagine YOU are the one who makes that show possible. Because I can't beat the Hollywood groomers without your help as a Founding Supporter. Season One of Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk has ten lesson-filled episodes planned. And each episode costs around $70,000 to produce. I know that's a lot of money but it's actually a fraction of what it would cost if we made our show with a major Hollywood studio. And obviously we won't work through Hollywood. Hollywood has done quite enough already to destroy American culture and hijack our children. We parents, grandparents, and concerned citizens must take over to rebuild a thriving, healthy, GOOD and GODLY culture. That's why I'm asking you to contribute $35, $50, $250, $500, or more to help us reach our fundraising goal of $70,000 per episode. Whether you can send $35, $50,000, or anything in between, or anything higher or lower -- please know that gifts of EVERY size are needed and greatly appreciated. As your special "Thank You" for joining me as a Founding Supporter at the $35 and above level, we'll send you all ten episodes of Adventures with Iggy and Mr. Kirk for your family to enjoy FREE on DVD. Hollywood groomers have destroyed America's Godly culture. But today we rebuild from the ashes. And it starts by ensuring the most innocent among us are filled with faith and family -- not filth. Thank you for joining us as a Founding Supporter. Kirk Cameron Actor, Author, and Father Kirk Cameron Actor, Author, and Father DONATE Brave Books 13618 Poplar Cir, Suite 302 Conroe, TX 77304 This message was intended for: dpwillis67@gmail.com You were added to the system January 18, 2022. # Monday, December 2, 2024
---|---
**Good morning!** On this day in 1907, citizens of Peck [renamed their community Tomball](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0z7FugzAQANCvsbcg39mJzeChC1u6dkSGu4BVgyM4BfL3Vbv3B570vu79PeWS1-kzLRzlfNQeDboL4MVgTymXd-80xcFQC5ojeGvBtmBveo4jgEfryQIAhRAc3dLoDQJfBzMA6Rx_MUCDABjw2jgerWkDsfWeQxiVM0xZ6tbIzMJn2h_lXV-8NWNddImzyHNX9kNhp7A7jqORfU51LXnlpm6Twm5OKw21fivseJUt866wk7oMqZSLnHqL9DxyKXm_eeXMtKRc_nT5t_uK-BMAAP__gA1XpQ) in honor of Thomas Henry Ball, a congressman and an advocate for the Houston Ship Channel. When oil was discovered nearby in 1933, up to 30 drilling companies descended on Tomball, which became known as “Oil Town USA.” If you spent hours in Thanksgiving traffic this past week, you might be surprised to hear that delays are down from five years ago. At least, that's what a report out of Texas A&M claims. Check out [our coverage](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00DGPhCAQBeBfg50GBly0oLjGbq-90szCqCQoRibr-u8vXn_ty8uXl_fzHJ8YU9zmb1zJ8WfKI0gwtYJawhgwpms0VXAvGXpVkVNWa6V7pR_V4ow0FqbgUUnUxlsyRNa2jxBeofOqraK7MQUSlIIO2saQ17LvAmlrqeu8MJJC5Hw0vBDTB8uUrvymo_F5rZJbmPci9JeAQcBwnmezXgU33DhvMd8lAcNGZxEwpOwxCRj4wGmKXsCAB0ef6M5uuua81z5vMxWmUB8Zw4lXqVXft9Iq2-zLXh0u7GdMKZaHFUbOK8b0N4b_feft4DcAAP__ER1oRw) below! We’d like to introduce our newest sponsor from right here in Arlington, Texas: [ _GreatNuts.com_](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0j7FugzAQQL_G3oLO58PYg4cubOnaETncBSyZEIEbkr-v0j37e096P-fhnHLJt-k7LRLr87oOCEgngyfAgVMur4E0xwtwMFqi6aw1Nhjr9BwdJHAUKAS2KNA6Yg8tpvbCxIGCzvEdMwhoDHpsG5LRQvAstuvE-1ERCOe6bk2dpcoz7dfyWh-yNeO66BLnWu-7sl8Ke4X9cRzNtEmqt9-6vwmFvd4i349cSt5dpwimJeXyb9ePO4-IfwEAAP__vNFLgw).

## Residents Divided Over Krampus Parade
Plans for a parade celebrating a character from German folklore [have sparked opposition](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kDGPtCAYhH8NdBh4QcGC4mvs9muvNCiobw7BCLfu_vuLl2uvnczMkzwfj_HhMGJa_7s92Ppa8ggcFBPAOIzeYXyPino7cd8LGqzQUgrZC9nRzbZGSy4lX5Q2ZtJd6ycAo3o1dRx8zyna-0wAByHAQNuoMEveGx-k1sGYmSgePNZ8NnULNbxcWeI7P8PZzHmn0W61HoXIfwQGAsN1XU0KV1HFJZdqTpjvHoHhTgkMMc8uEhg-T7cfX4VhYXPeMa2sZlZcYr8relp_XBgjlk4TxdfdYfxB1j8dPC18BwAA__-yGV7P) from some San Antonio residents. Krampus is a mythical half-goat, half-demon figure who, according to tradition, sends misbehaving children to Hell during the holidays. The [parade featuring Krampus](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00L2OgzAQBOCnMZ2Rd81v4eIad7n2SmTwAisMjrAvJG9_SvqrP2lmND-34eY48LF8u51Mfs5xQIWVBJQKB-84vIaq8GZUvoeCDLRag-5BN8VqNNSk62Ye66Zvpll70H3b1gB6Bu99X7B5hwEqBMAO67KiSau-86TblrpuEpUizzmeZV4p09OlObzig85yinsRzJrzPQn9JdAKtNd1lVty-Y0C7UFXEmhDnFwQaN9VAi2AQKuVQLudbr__JvlIMjk-sjx42qSn0WWSKfOZJB9y42ORF4fAbpcH8bKO8Vxj9AJtcRp__1hqWlGpZXccPtPyv189DP4FAAD__ySVawc) is scheduled to march through the King William neighborhood in San Antonio this Thursday evening. While organizers say the parade is simply an effort to celebrate German culture, other residents, such as Mark Lugo, pastor of San Antonio Impact Ministries, [aren't too keen about it](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LGO6yAQheGnwZ0tGEgMBcVt3OW2W1oTGMdosUHAxvHbr5J--6NPR__Xbb5hiGF__MeNbHstaQYOqhfQc5g9hnjOqvP2zr0RHVkxSimkEfLarVZod0FOZpFXIwHFopArA9rfpZZGYRfsGxPAQQjQcBkUOcmN9iTHkbR2THHyoaUytJUavbAu8UxPKoNLWxft2lquTP5jMDGYjuMYtrPijntLe0jvEYNpp6MymGJyGBlMWFpwkRhM3wW3_FP7jAU99R-_z1hbKr0wRmkDlyGvuSvW5yPEGOp1ZIo_Ngzx86D9meRp4TcAAP__6aJjqQ). “ _We’re saying that this fear-mongering is not allowed in this city. Krampus is not welcome in San Antonio_ ,” Lugo said.
## New State Park Opening Next Summer
Palo Pinto Mountains State Park is set to open next summer on 4,871 acres of former ranch land 75 miles west of Fort Worth. It will be the [first new state park in Texas in 25 years](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0z7uOwyAQheGnwR3WzIBtKCi2cZdtt7SImcRo8UUYheTtV0m_9ZG-o__nMl18THG7f_uVXXne9omAtESSQFPwMb0m3QR3hWCxYYeDUqgsqr5ZHNvedoCWsO-UhatiMl3goK9swBjVRPfGkIAQyVDXap4VWBNYDQMbMwsNHGLZc1sWLvz05y299gfndt7XJrmllOMU6kvQKGistbbzkvftvQoaN66noNHnEufEgsaPIDeu8iy-sDx8_pVorbZgdHssR5NdOGpMKZ79IDTcVx_T56v8G_9w9BcAAP__2Q5atw). The park was supposed to open in 2023 but was [postponed due to construction delays](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kDGPhCAUhH8Ndhp4oEJBcY3dXnul4QDXlyAYebuu__6y2187mcx8-X5u881hwnz_dlu09FrKDBxUK6DlMAeH6ZpVE-wvD0Y00YpRSiGNkEOz2h6C1wuM0SuvR6XDIPohBANDr0YhXYP2PSaAgxCgoe9U9JIbHaIcx6i1Z4rHgFSOjtZI8eXqkq7yjEfny9YkuxLtlckvBhOD6TzPbruqyy5TyVjeJQZTwiVWulJkMJUHhVKOymByB6H_hLtLpd0xU2m38sjkMNf289YKY5Thg-72dW8OG_YTU8I6jEzx--YwfUDoXzNPC38BAAD__yPPZus). With 60 camping sites and 25 RV pads, the park will offer hiking, biking, horseback riding, fishing, paddling, bird watching, and stargazing to its expected 75,000 to 100,000 annual visitors. Although Palo Pinto is relatively close to a major population center, it still [has a “Wild West” feel](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx0kL1uwyAUhZ8Gb9eCi38Hhiyemo7taBFzHaNisIDYcZ--SvaOR0c659P3fR2v2jrr7596JZWfcxiRYwUCgeNotHXnWBVG3bjpRUFKtFIK2QvZFIsiw-f6piXVbSMbrLu64XwyXXUjLrnuC6teYwI5CoEd1mVFk-R9Z0i2LXXdxCpOxuYQy7xQpqdOszvDTrGcwlo4teS8JSYvDAeGw3Ec5Zr8q2M4kIdHYjjkqHdyDAdPxytvliaCMMNinYMpPHyOJ_gQ8wLvC5htTBk8HZCyzgSbjj9gPWANJ-mYIFKinCBs5K2_g9GZGA46wuUiHl_7x28RldkO65xNTcsqfl-1dW_m_K_EXeFfAAAA___PYnYo), according to park superintendent James Adams. Deer, porcupines, and bobcats have been spotted within the park.
## The Top 100 Most Congested Roadways in Texas
A recent report from the Texas A&M Transportation Institute shows the [top 100 most congested roadways](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00LGO8yAQBOCnwZ0RLI4xBcXfuMvfXmlh2CQrgYnMOpe8_SkpT7pymplP83VezoEybdf_oaDn56UuoGDoNfQKlhQov5ahS35VyekOvbbGaOO0Gbubj6eY1tWFNI5rdG5ap3CKYC2ADhG07ci_yzQo0BomOMkBo1FuSmisxWmKYlCYiOsu-YaMz9Au-VUfuMtYS5f9jfnehPknYBYwNw5MUTKT5FAOiekQMP-Kqcaj4MZNwHwUEjCX2riPdbtiY0w9bf1nqX_b5D1dut2n-zflTG20YlDXEih_APznIw8PPwEAAP__KdhkCw) in Texas. Unsurprisingly, highways in Dallas and Houston top the list, with a stretch of I-35 through downtown Austin coming in third. Drivers spent 1,370,786 person-hours per mile on a 3.62-mile segment of I-610 in Houston this year, amounting to an annual economic cost of $125,922,628. A 1.44-mile stretch of Dallas' Central Expressway was second on the list, costing drivers 1,342,429 person-hours per mile and $47,816,081 in lost time and fuel. Congestion on a [7.93-mile segment of I-35 through Austin](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00DGPhCAQBeBfg50GBly0oLjGbq-90szCqCQoRibr-u8vXn_ty8uXl_fzHJ8YU9zmb1zJ8WfKI0gwtYJawhgwpms0VXAvGXpVkVNWa6V7pR_V4ow0FqbgUUnUxlsyRNa2jxBeofOqraK7MQUSlIIO2saQ17LvAmlrqeu8MJJC5Hw0vBDTB8uUrvymo_F5rZJbmPci9JeAQcBwnmezXgU33DhvMd8lAcNGZxEwpOwxCRj4wGmKXsCAB0ef6M5uuua81z5vMxWmUB8Zw4lXqVXft9Iq2-zLXh0u7GdMKZaHFUbOK8b0N4b_feft4DcAAP__ER1oRw) has the highest annual economic cost, a whopping $197,166,266. The report found that while the average driver’s miles traveled increased by 7% in the last five years, [delays actually decreased](http://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJx00L1uwyAUxfGnwRsW9wI2Hhi6sKVrR4vAdYKEAzLk6-2rdO9-dH7S_-e0nnzK6Xb59jvZ_trKigIVB-QC1-hTfq9qiPYs4gIDWZilBLmAnIarNdO2aB-iWAJorZWWXkM8hzgpI2XAIdnPGaBAADSoR0VBisVEkvNMxgSmBMXUyzH2K3V6-bbld3nQMYayD9lee6-NyS-GjqHbyznl1N9j9_t9pHhn6J6Vh3LrdOsM3b3m4mNj6D4uQwfA0NWDWuMHZfKNeC-VgxD8sxhr3IbDxvpMOac2zUyJy-5T_uP7vz0eFn8DAAD__wwyYGQ), saving those drivers an estimated $915 million in time and fuel.

Krampus is a mythical half-goat, half-demon figure who, according to tradition, sends misbehaving children to Hell during the holidays. The is scheduled to march through the King William neighborhood in San Antonio this Thursday evening. While organizers say the parade is simply an effort to celebrate German culture, other residents, such as Mark Lugo, pastor of San Antonio Impact Ministries, . “ We’re saying that this fear-mongering is not allowed in this city. Krampus is not welcome in San Antonio ,” Lugo said. New State Park Opening Next Summer Palo Pinto Mountains State Park is set to open next summer on 4,871 acres of former ranch land 75 miles west of Fort Worth. It will be the . The park was supposed to open in 2023 but was . With 60 camping sites and 25 RV pads, the park will offer hiking, biking, horseback riding, fishing, paddling, bird watching, and stargazing to its expected 75,000 to 100,000 annual visitors. Although Palo Pinto is relatively close to a major population center, it still , according to park superintendent James Adams. Deer, porcupines, and bobcats have been spotted within the park. The Top 100 Most Congested Roadways in Texas A recent report from the Texas A&M Transportation Institute shows the in Texas. Unsurprisingly, highways in Dallas and Houston top the list, with a stretch of I-35 through downtown Austin coming in third. Drivers spent 1,370,786 person-hours per mile on a 3.62-mile segment of I-610 in Houston this year, amounting to an annual economic cost of $125,922,628. A 1.44-mile stretch of Dallas' Central Expressway was second on the list, costing drivers 1,342,429 person-hours per mile and $47,816,081 in lost time and fuel. Congestion on a has the highest annual economic cost, a whopping $197,166,266. President Joe Biden [pardoned his son Hunter](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL1uwyAUhZ8Gb1hw8Q8ZGLp4S9eOFobr-EYELCCx8vaVqw5duh3pOzo6-r6u89VSoHj7tA80dU0zCOi4BC5g9pbCe-4ab1ZEQNWgkaNSUl2U1s1m1KXTyyAQLfQ9rM47dFp4i2JRvpdjQ-ZckyBASjXIscVFu7X3Z3P0o5asE-ipptzeE8W64Rre6YW5denRBLPVuhemPhhMDKbjONq4uIhHOTmDaU-BKrnCYLon5At5jH8zp1KeyHebfYq8pMi3Z6yYf2l20UoNarg02fj9oBCoDCPrxO1hKfycqP9JeRn4DgAA___MtmXL) Sunday night after promising he wouldn’t use his executive authority to pardon or commute his son’s conviction on federal gun charges.

## Biden Gives Surprise Pardon to Son Hunter
President Joe Biden [pardoned his son Hunter](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kL1uwyAUhZ8Gb1hw8Q8ZGLp4S9eOFobr-EYELCCx8vaVqw5duh3pOzo6-r6u89VSoHj7tA80dU0zCOi4BC5g9pbCe-4ab1ZEQNWgkaNSUl2U1s1m1KXTyyAQLfQ9rM47dFp4i2JRvpdjQ-ZckyBASjXIscVFu7X3Z3P0o5asE-ipptzeE8W64Rre6YW5denRBLPVuhemPhhMDKbjONq4uIhHOTmDaU-BKrnCYLon5At5jH8zp1KeyHebfYq8pMi3Z6yYf2l20UoNarg02fj9oBCoDCPrxO1hKfycqP9JeRn4DgAA___MtmXL) Sunday night after promising he wouldn’t use his executive authority to pardon or commute his son’s conviction on federal gun charges. [In a statement accompanying the pardon](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kDGPhCAUhH8Ndhh4oLgFxTV0e-2VBuWpb4OyAW7N_vuLV11z3UzyZfJlvu7j3VOkY_30O9q6pBEEaC6BCxiDp_gedRPsggioGrTSKCXVTQ1Ds1nth85ofYNeDFIpE7q5Az-FIHwP3W1qyF5rEgRIqXppWpyGeenCHGY0wQySaYGBasrtI9FRN1ziO70wt3Pam2i3Wp-FqQ8GjoE7z7M9N6q4pe-C7ZpeDNyUCRc6Vp5T2hm4Un3FHY9aeMaIvmBh4C4NBk4CAyfkX4wvOe38mbFQuOojIZ-uyOXFNdmG50kxUukN02LdPcVfu_rfWy8LPwEAAP__zAdsIQ), Biden blamed Hunter’s conviction on his political enemies. _“Hunter was singled out only because he was my son—and that is wrong,”_ Biden said. Both David Weiss, the special counsel prosecuting the case, and Hunter Biden’s lawyer Abbe Lowell declined to comment. The White House said as recently as last Monday that the president wouldn’t pardon his son. Biden said he believed in the justice system but that [his son’s conviction was “infected”](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00LFu6yAUgOGngY0IDmBzB4a7eEvXjhY2x-FUGCKgifL2Vbp3_qVv-D-v6zVQpnL7CCf6cdQVJBihQEhYY6D8Wg2P_kAE1By9mrVW-p92jic_xU3aYzfGGQub3SEqoy3o2WkM22Y4-bemQIJSelLzBTe3Hzbuccc5zk4xIzHSqO3yVamMhEd-1Qe2y15Pnn0a496Z_s9gYbCMhIlyfjcGS6onFnx2BkuIJxXqo4VBtTBYrFSTs0psFLGIe2ixli4SddFrEem7DGwMFt58vD8pZ-rTzIy8nYF-eT7-WvHw8BMAAP__5FBhAg) by “raw politics,” adding _“I hope Americans will understand why a father and a President would come to this decision.”_
## Trump Taps Former Aide for FBI Chief
President-elect Donald Trump announced his intention to name former White House aide [Kash Patel as FBI Director](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0j7tuxCAUBb8GOiy4YOMUFGncbdqUFo_LmgQbC8ha-_fRpk93pCPNaD5v682mnI77h93R9FhW4KCYAMZhDTbl56poMBERUFI0Qksp5JucZ7oZ6UYeoufgXbB81M5HFZ0SM45-9NNEk3nRBHAQQk5CD-hmH8fgg0cd9CyI4hhSL3X4KunoG8b8LA-sgy87zWbr_WxEvhNYCCzXdQ3etQOv9voJLK9JYOn1Zz9ZL-ywO7Jv2zZ22o6Z2caiSyykir6XSmCh1YTzSjmnNmmi-H23Kf_Z-n_1DwO_AQAA___-_14I), calling the 44-year-old lawyer an “‘America First’ fighter.” Patel, a former national security prosecutor and chief of staff to the acting defense secretary, drew [praise from Speaker Mike Johnson](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLuOwyAQRb8GOiLPgB8pKLZxl223tHgMgQQby7CJ8verpN_uSlc6Rzo_l-ViUk7b9duspFsoC3aoBKDocPEm5deiuNeBCEly0jBKCfIsp4lH3RsLNPRBWgsKlHVBTR7PNvTeBzOMPOk3DbBDADnAeCI7udB75x2NfpyAqY58auU43UraWqSQX-VBx8mVlWcdW9srk18MZ4ZzixRTzu-P4RzLShs962f-VmI49x0MCntRdzJ3OsStxK2WTVjj7lXcTY1iN42yCOUQwSaGMz-0358p51SHkanuupr0MfD2X42Hxr8AAAD__7gRYo8) and other Republicans. He faced criticism over his [limited law enforcement experience](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00L1u7CAQxfGnwR2WZ2ANW1Dcxt3eNqWFmWFNgj-EyVr79pHTpz7S_yedj8f48Cmn9fnfL-xq3EbsUEtA2eFIPuX3qBtykRlZNezAKAXqrqxtZhfv3pANcVIa-r4nD9rYKXpLN9QhUJPcVQPsEED1YFqebIg3ChTYkLEgdMeU6lbazy2tdeaY39uLSxu2pclurnU_hPoncBA4nOfZhnUK1yhwuNICBwCBg-oEDrV8L7usfj_klz9mufvKWcatyDglSalwuKS5LrkpjvYz5ZyO3gjdPRef8i9a_zrh5fAnAAD__4ukXlo) from some in that field. If confirmed by the Senate, Patel would replace Chris Wray, appointed by Trump in 2017, whose term runs until 2027 unless removed.
## Football Conference Championships Set
College football’s [conference championship games](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kLHOozAQBp8GOiJ218GmcHFNulx7JVrWa_DJYARWorz9Kdf_5adPGo3mz3N6csppX37zpr7GMmGPpgPsepwCp_yZTBt8VEWlVj1YIqCRnGtXfx-DOKPsnDGAQjqSYVB19x5kDHOb_JcG2CMADWBvOjuJ9yBB1AbroDG9hlTLeftb0l5XjflTXnrepGxt9mutx9XQrwYfDT742PV9fa_vOGuSrA0-pOSsi3axlDpzzp2svB2p7Neajm7hTa-OzUwRREc7QwQyOgrpEAiJZysDt6cPxzvlnK7BNqZfNk75v0T9KcrL478AAAD__wJ0ZBM) are set after a wild, brawl-filled rivalry weekend. Ohio State’s fourth straight loss to rival Michigan, a stunning 13-10 upset, paved the way for Penn State to take on undefeated Oregon in the Big Ten title game and for a [post-game brawl](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx00DFvwyAUBOBfg7dEfg_bwMDQpmVLMrabhc3DocLBwjRu_n2VqGunk77hdLqPY3-0IYbrdLIz6eJTjzU2O8Bdjb2zId77pnLaEyHxijQIzoErLmV10YNQEtTgFFh0ygrrXOcBu9o7AAtDFfSjDbBGAN6B2NMgR9-60Y0knJDAmppcKCnvv1K4lgv5eE83yvsxzVXUl1KWlfEXhoah-XkoQ3Mwr-eTOX8yNGux5XtlaEB2qDoUgJ0SdctbqRg3mXy_5pHxt7I9Etv2fUppisSwFYeyjXZenrpSXv5syk8pG1GpsnbLFmIMaydYU0-zDfE5rvx31k3jbwAAAP__nVRn1A) when Michigan players planted a flag on the Buckeyes’ field. Other conference matchups: Georgia and Texas will face off in the SEC championship game in Atlanta; Syracuse’ upset of Miami gave an ACC championship berth to Clemson for a matchup with SMU; and Iowa State and Arizona State play for the Big 12 title in Arlington, Texas. [Here’s a rundown](http://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJx0kD-vpSAUxD8Ndhg4-Leg2IbubrulOcJB2aAY4V3jt3_xvvpVM8XkN5P595peGGLYl7-4kS4-TSCg4RK4gMlhiPfUVE57IiBVkZa9UlKNahiqVc8OSQqvrFWALdp29J6wG2F0vnUtVUE_NAkCpFSd7GuaB-tbZ52l3vWDZI0gF0o66_8p7GUlH-_0prO2aauiXks5MlN_GBgG5rqu-itjSQ7vJ8DA5JLO-9EjnSUzMLtF9AzM08vASGBghGRgbIqRFuI-pTJjjNym3dNJuyVuV9yOkPa8hoMvuFHmmSyfw8IL7Rztj__A-q4b2rZTQvQMTHVqd1whxpC7njVi2TDEz_jy25lvDd8BAAD__73zdgU) of every conference championship game, all scheduled for this coming Saturday, Dec. 7.

subject: They’ve seized all my money

body:
But even so, I will never surrender the fight for freedom and justice.

[https://secure.winred.com/rudy-giuliani-freedom-fund-79a3adfa/497966903baa536c/?utm_campaign=20241119&utm_medium=email&utm_source=RW_B&utm_content=WAMA&utm_term=vbout]

Friend,

Imagine being asked to give up your home, your car, your credit card,
and your checking account.

Imagine all of your money being seized.

_That’s what the Communist Democrats have done to me._

I am being politically persecuted all because I dared to ask
questions... I dared to speak the TRUTH...

As I continue to fight for my freedom and justice, I need the greatest
legal team in history to win against the Marxist Deep State —
especially since my own law licenses have been _taken away_ from me!

But there’s one thing I want to make clear...

...The media claims that I am “begging” for donations to my Legal
Defense Trust.

_THAT’S A LIE. _

The truth is: when I ask for contributions to my Legal Defense Trust,
I tell Americans NOT to donate if they can’t afford it. But of
course the media and the Democrats will never report that.

And I’ll say it again loud and clear. If you can’t afford to
contribute, please don’t. End of story.

But, if you can, please know that your support could be the key to my
freedom – and not just _my own_ freedom, but the freedom, justice,
and rights of every U.S. citizen.

PLEASE MAKE A CONTRIBUTION TO THE RUDY GIULIANI FREEDOM FUND, BECAUSE
EVEN AFTER EVERYTHING THE LEFT HAS DONE TO ME, I WILL _NEVER_

name: Hillsdale College via Your America News
subject: Bah! Humbug!
body:
Charles Dickens, a very great novelist, had a special gift for revealing important truths in delightful ways

This sponsored message from Your American Update is on behalf of Hillsdale College

| | | [](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zune50pb4thqovg9cdbp2kh660mkmoeps92746s0_rp22sh2s8i6epj6chim1o72c9h2bs)
---
| |
Dear Friend,

Charles Dickens, a very great novelist, had a special gift for revealing important truths in delightful ways through unforgettable characters.

To mark this holiday season, Hillsdale College released an online course on what is perhaps Dickens' most beloved novel: _A Christmas Carol_.

The course is taught by the excellent Dwight Lindley, an English professor here at Hillsdale who loves both reading Dickens and teaching Dickens.

In this six-lecture course, Dwight will help you understand more fully the causes of Ebenezer Scrooge's conversion in the novel and what the story teaches us about leading a happy and complete life.

You can use the secure link below to begin experiencing the wisdom and fun of _A Christmas Carol_ today.

**[https://online.hillsdale.edu/courses/register/christmas-carol](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z2gl7hvkta4c4d32e7na4co3hdm7p1ut912ih6eo_rp22sh2s8i6epj6chim1o72c9h2bs)**

Let your family and friends know of this free online course as well. We hope it will add meaning and joy to your Christmas celebrations.

Best regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

---

Your America
---
_PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zilvpl493782ri8sqkmsqai14pppdfo696njloqg_rp22sh2s8i6epj6chim1o72c9h238h5e8he485he5o75pt7kuo72ttle5pq9ttle9q3bd9ieir38ct3mkrnctt5eer7ashmeop79chl6ujuadf161rn9cdhmir76pr02sqr8d5hmej3fddj4iqu8pj3ctim9pj24no)_ | _[manage data preferences](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zoanvt5v5j7n1fnca3ul734gi78hfg603ce1arv8_rp22sh2s8i6epj6chim1o72c9h2bs)_

[](http://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2z5frm2qd98hfu5lnvcmjlsusbe9nf226vb8u2m9o_rp22sh2s8i6epj6chim1o72c9h238h5e8he49h666qbcdli6uon49fg)

name: History Facts
subject: The inventor of dynamite will surprise you
body:
Since its inception in 1901, the Nobel Prize has been synonymous with scientific and artistic genius. [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc68cmtc6vh1xt15l06wn449h0lv8orvu5kcbj0rxc6cy6kqcebggjodyto4iks5pmrlsju9i8vd02qghzt2zu0ihksx4t26up1ijmtl24brl80i9hy3pmeeufn9kh5c4o1boc3yp6m9vgnbr1q&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snv897&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

# [Dynamite was invented by the namesake of the Nobel Prize.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snv897&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [Science & Industry](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snv897&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snv897&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| S ince its inception in 1901, the Nobel Prize has been synonymous with scientific and artistic genius. However, many people don’t realize it was named after the Swedish chemist who [invented dynamite](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snv897&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) in 1867, Alfred Nobel. The powerful explosive was one of the most consequential inventions of the Industrial Revolution, transforming the way large-scale infrastructure projects such as roads, canals, and bridges were built. Dynamite was not only more potent than previous forms of explosives, but also more stable and easier to control, making it an ideal substance for efficiently blasting through mountains, earth, and other obstacles.
---
The invention of dynamite and several related substances made Nobel a very wealthy man: By the time of his death in 1896, he had amassed what was at the time one of the world’s largest private fortunes. In his will, he stipulated that the majority of his personal wealth should be invested in a fund of stable securities and apportioned out every year in the form of prizes recognizing individuals who had [“conferred the greatest benefit on mankind.”](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snv897&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) The prizes were awarded in five categories: physics, chemistry, physiology or medicine, literature, and (perhaps ironically for a man whose fortune came from explosives and weaponry) peace. The first Nobel Prizes were awarded in 1901, five years after the namesake inventor’s death. In 1968, Sweden’s central bank introduced a sixth Nobel Prize category, economics, which is now awarded annually alongside the five original categories dictated by Nobel’s will.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snv897&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snv897&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)

## Alfred Nobel had to flee France after being accused of high treason.
In 1881, Alfred Nobel purchased a large house with an adjacent laboratory in [Sevran](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=snv897&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com), a French village northeast of Paris, where he continued his research into ballistics and explosives. Most significantly, Nobel’s work at Sevran led to the invention of ballistite, a form of gunpowder that was nearly smokeless upon combustion. The Italian government recognized the power of ballistite and purchased a large amount of the gunpowder from Nobel for military use, but when the French government learned that Nobel, a French resident, had sold this powerful new weapons technology to a foreign military, they accused Nobel of high treason. They also implied that Nobel had developed ballistite through espionage by spying on the French military and stealing the formula for a type of gunpowder called Poudre B, another smokeless gunpowder that was developed around the same time but relied on an entirely different chemical process. In the face of these false accusations, Nobel was forced to flee France in 1891 and relocate to Italy, where he lived for the last five years of his life.
